channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CDC Investigates Cyclospora Outbreak Affecting Over 400 People Across Four States
item.description: A growing Cyclospora outbreak has infected over 400 individuals across four Midwestern states, prompting the CDC to collaborate with federal and state health agencies to identify the contaminated food...

item.title: South Africa rejects claims of isolation over immigration policies, accuses Ghana of misinformation
item.description: The South African government has accused Ghana of spearheading a coordinated campaign to isolate the country from the African continent, citing tensions over its strict immigration policies. Presidenc...

item.title: Billy Joel's 'Just the Way You Are' Nearly Excluded from Album Thanks to Linda Ronstadt's Influence
item.description: Billy Joel has revealed that his iconic song 'Just the Way You Are' almost did not make it onto his 1977 album The Stranger. Initially, the artist doubted its potential, dismissing it as 'too soft' an...

item.title: Congress outlines Parliament strategy, vows to oppose Delimitation Bill and raise corruption, inflation and governance issues during Monsoon Session
item.description: The Congress party has announced its strategy for the upcoming Monsoon Session of Parliament, stating that it will aggressively question the Narendra Modi government on a wide range of political, econ...

item.title: DOE Commits to Ongoing Support for PUV Drivers Amid Mideast Tensions
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has assured PUV (Public Utility Vehicle) drivers of continued financial assistance despite heightened tensions in the Middle East. This announcement comes as global mark...

item.title: New Exhibit Honors Racing Legend Gilles Villeneuve
item.description: Nearly 45 years after his passing, Gilles Villeneuve remains a source of inspiration for racing enthusiasts. A new exhibition at Parc Jean-Drapeau showcases the life and career of the Quebec icon, hig...

item.title: Senator urges transparency in Ol Kalou by-election security
item.description: Senator John Methu has called on security agencies to ensure identifiable police officers are deployed at polling stations during the Ol Kalou by-election, warning against the use of hooded officers. ...

item.title: Three suspects face bail hearing in R15m gemstone theft case
item.description: Three individuals arrested by the Independent Police Investigative Directorate (IPID) are set to appear in Johannesburg magistrate’s court on July 16, 2026, for a formal bail application. The suspects...

item.title: Norway Funds Digital Accountability Project to Enhance Public Transparency in Tanzania
item.description: The Norwegian Embassy in Tanzania has committed TZS1.8 billion (NOK 7 million) to the 'Wajibika Kidigitali' project, aimed at improving public financial accountability through digital platforms. This ...

item.title: Barbara Stanwyck's 1940s Hollywood Career and Iconic Style
item.description: Barbara Stanwyck was at the peak of her Hollywood stardom in the 1940s, becoming one of the era’s most versatile and respected actresses. Known for her strong screen presence and professionalism, she ...

item.title: New AI regulations face challenge of aligning tech giants with global standards
item.description: The article explores the challenges of implementing a unified AI regulatory framework that can effectively govern multinational technology companies. With major firms like Google, Meta, and Amazon ope...

item.title: Mysticeti's 'Bapa Wants Fish' Explores Family, Food, and Mental Health Through a Child's Lens
item.description: The Varanasi-based art collective Mysticeti, founded by Stuti Sareen, has expanded into children's publishing with 'Bapa Wants Fish', a limited-edition book inspired by 1990s Odisha. The story, struct...

item.title: 28-Year-Old Man Gets 7-Year Sentence for Social Media Cannabis Ad
item.description: A 28-year-old man, Ashiru Idris, has been sentenced to seven years in prison for advertising and selling cannabis sativa on social media. The Federal High Court in Kano convicted Idris after he was ar...

item.title: Apple TV's 'Lucky' Fails to Deliver Despite Strong Cast
item.description: The review of Apple TV's 'Lucky' highlights the series' struggles with plot coherence and character development despite a talented cast. The seven-episode limited series, adapted from Jonathan Tropper...

item.title: Government Enhances Security Measures for Chinese Mine Amid Terror Threats
item.description: The Pakistani government has announced increased security measures around a Chinese-operated copper and gold mine in Balochistan following recent terror attacks that disrupted supply routes. The Saind...

item.title: Congress seeks Centre's response to US's 100% tariff proposal on India for Russian oil imports
item.description: The Indian National Congress has requested Union Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal to clarify the Centre's stance following a bipartisan US Senate proposal to impose 100% tariffs on imports from India an...

item.title: Sammy Kamau Confident of Winning Ol Kalou By-Election
item.description: Democratic Congress Party (DCP) candidate Sammy Kamau has expressed confidence in winning the Ol Kalou parliamentary by-election, citing a strong campaign and voter support. Speaking after casting his...

item.title: Pakistan Mediates Peace Talks Between US and Iran Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: The Foreign Office of Pakistan has called on the United States and Iran to end violence and resume technical-level talks, amid ongoing hostilities between the two nations. Spokesperson Tahir Andrabi h...

item.title: Telus World of Science Closes Due to Flooding, Leads to Staff Layoffs
item.description: The Telus World of Science Edmonton (TWOSE) has been forced to close its doors for several months due to severe flooding damage, resulting in the temporary layoff of 68% of its staff. The closure foll...

item.title: Systemic Exclusion of Youth from Formal Politics in Tanzania
item.description: Young Tanzanians are increasingly alienated from formal political processes due to a culture that demands unquestioning loyalty and systematically excludes them from decision-making. The article highl...
